summary: Stopped the in-cluster SeaweedFS object-store pod on GKE after confirming the active cloud spill tier is GCS.

actions.result: Merged noetl/ops#77 adding objectStore.replicas for SeaweedFS and RustFS
Helm release noetl revision 131 set objectStore.replicas=0
deployment/seaweedfs is 0/0, no app=seaweedfs pods are running, service/object-store and pvc/object-store-data remain present
noetl-worker config still uses NOETL_STORAGE_CLOUD_TIER=gcs with NOETL_GCS_BUCKET=noetl-demo-output
Added re-provision and stop-again runbook to the GKE deployment drift and storage tier audit issue

re_enable_summary: helm upgrade noetl automation/helm/noetl --reuse-values --set objectStore.enabled=true --set objectStore.kind=seaweedfs --set objectStore.replicas=1
if routing cloud spillover back to S3, set worker/server NOETL_STORAGE_CLOUD_TIER=s3 and restart NoETL
